changelog.title: System Changelog | CS-Arena
changelog.description: Track the evolution of the CS-Arena ecosystem and our latest deployments.
changelog.subtitle: Witness the relentless progress of our platform. Every commit, every fix, and every feature is a step toward the ultimate developer ecosystem.
changelog.empty_sub: No updates logged yet. Our engineers are likely building something massive right now.

open_source.title: Open Source | CS-Arena
open_source.hero_subtitle: CS-Arena is more than code; it's a living ecosystem. We believe in radical transparency and community-driven innovation. Every line of code here belongs to the builders.
open_source.card1_desc: Take a copy of the Arena, experiment with its architecture, and build your own vision or help us improve ours.
open_source.card2_desc: Got a fix or a new feature? Our CI/CD pipeline is ready for your PR. We review everything within 24 hours.
open_source.card3_desc: Help us keep the Arena clean. Report issues, suggest optimizations, and ensure stability for thousands of students.
open_source.card4_desc: Boost our visibility in the global tech community. A star is a vote for the future of student-led platforms.
open_source.hall_subtitle: The visionary developers who helped architect the Arena.
open_source.cta_desc: Dive into the source code, explore our stack, and leave your mark on the next generation of CS tools.

about.title: About CS-Arena
about.description: Discover the vision, mission, and technology behind CS-Arena.
about.hero_subtitle: We are not just a project sharing platform. CS-Arena is the definitive hub for Computer Science students to showcase talent, compete globally, and get hired by top tech recruiters.
about.mission_desc: Bridging the gap between academic learning and industry demands. We provide a competitive, open-source environment where every line of code you write counts towards your global and university ranking. We don't just host your projects; we amplify your tech career.
about.feature1_desc: Compete not just locally, but globally. Our dynamic ranking system positions you based on your code quality, upvotes, and impact.
about.feature2_desc: A dedicated space connecting visionary project leads with eager talent. Find your next co-founder, or get scouted by tech recruiters.
about.feature3_desc: Every project is a stepping stone. Share, collaborate, fork, and build upon the collective knowledge of the CS-Arena community.
about.tech_desc: CS-Arena handles real-time interactions, massive data rendering, and complex queries seamlessly. We've engineered this platform using the bleeding edge of web technology to ensure zero latency and maximum developer experience.
about.cta_desc: Join thousands of elite developers. Claim your rank, deploy your ideas, and let your code speak for itself.

privacy.title: Privacy & Security Policy | CS-Arena
privacy.description: How we protect your data, code, and career profile in the Arena.
privacy.hero_subtitle: In the Arena, your data is your most valuable asset. We treat your code, personal information, and recruitment profile with military-grade security and absolute transparency.
privacy.trust_card1_desc: Your open-source projects remain 100% yours. We claim zero ownership over your repositories.
privacy.trust_card2_desc: You control who sees your contact info. Toggle your 'Actively Hiring' visibility anytime.
privacy.trust_card3_desc: All sensitive data and credentials are encrypted using AES-256 protocols at rest and in transit.
privacy.intro: Welcome to the CS-Arena Security and Privacy Center. This document outlines exactly how your information is handled when you step into the ecosystem.
privacy.section1_intro: To provide the best competitive experience and matchmaking with recruiters, we collect necessary telemetry:
privacy.section1_list1: Profile Data: GitHub stats, university affiliation, and chosen domains.
privacy.section1_list2: Project Metrics: Upvotes, views, and tech stack usage for the Leaderboard.
privacy.section1_list3: Recruitment Flags: Your 'Looking for team' or 'Available for hire' status.
privacy.section2_intro: Your data is strictly used to enhance your presence in the Arena:
privacy.section2_list1: Ranking algorithms for the Global and University Leaderboards.
privacy.section2_list2: Connecting you with verified tech recruiters and project leads.
privacy.section2_list3: Preventing upvote manipulation and ensuring fair competition.
privacy.section2_outro: We will NEVER sell your data to third-party ad networks. Period.
privacy.section3_intro1: CS-Arena relies on world-class infrastructure provided by Sanity CMS and Vercel. Your data is routed through secure, distributed edge networks to ensure ultra-low latency.
privacy.section3_intro2: Authentication is handled entirely via NextAuth and OAuth providers (like GitHub). We do not store your raw passwords.
privacy.section4_intro: You are the master of your profile:
privacy.section4_list1: Right to Erasure (Delete your account and projects instantly).
privacy.section4_list2: Right to Anonymity (Hide your university or contact info).
privacy.section4_list3: Right to Export (Download your project metadata at any time).

cookies.title: Cookie Policy | CS-Arena
cookies.description: How we use cookies to keep the Arena secure and functional.
cookies.hero_subtitle: We hate tracking as much as you do. Our cookie policy is built on strict functionality and security, ensuring your session remains yours alone.
cookies.cookie_type1_desc: Authentication tokens and session IDs. Without these, you can't log in or deploy projects.
cookies.cookie_type2_desc: We measure page views and load times to optimize server performance. No personal tracking.
cookies.cookie_type3_desc: Saves your theme (Dark/Light) and language selection locally so you don't have to toggle it every time.
cookies.intro: As a platform built for developers, we believe in complete transparency regarding what is stored in your browser. Here is the exact breakdown.
cookies.section1_title: 1. What is a Cookie (in our context)?
cookies.section1_intro: We use the term 'Cookies' broadly to include:
cookies.section1_list1: HTTP Cookies: Secure, HttpOnly cookies managed by NextAuth for session handling.
cookies.section1_list2: Local Storage: Storing non-sensitive UI preferences (like your current tab in the Dashboard).
cookies.section2_intro: We keep third-party scripts to an absolute minimum to preserve performance and privacy:
cookies.section2_list1: OAuth Providers: If you log in with GitHub or Google, they may place cookies to verify your identity.
cookies.section2_list2: Sanity CMS: Used purely for fetching real-time data, not for tracking user behavior.
cookies.section3_intro: You are a developer; you know how to clear your cache. But here is our official stance:
cookies.section3_list1: You can block all cookies via your browser, but CS-Arena will fail to log you in.
cookies.section3_list2: We do not use 'Tracking Pixels' or aggressive re-targeting ads.
cookies.section3_list3: Logging out immediately destroys your active session tokens.
cookies.section3_outro: By continuing to use CS-Arena, you agree to our minimal and functional use of these local storage mechanisms.

terms.title: Terms of Service | CS-Arena
terms.description: The rules of engagement and code of conduct within the CS-Arena ecosystem.
terms.hero_subtitle: By deploying code, voting on projects, or recruiting talent in the Arena, you agree to these fundamental rules. We keep it simple, fair, and developer-first.
terms.tldr_1: You own your code. We just host the showcase.
terms.tldr_2: No malicious code, plagiarism, or spam. Keep it clean.
terms.tldr_3: Respect the community. Vote manipulation equals an instant ban.
terms.intro: These Terms of Service outline your rights and responsibilities when using CS-Arena. Please read them carefully.
terms.section1_desc: The Arena is for builders. By joining, you agree NOT to:
terms.section1_list1: Upload malware, viruses, or destructive code.
terms.section1_list2: Plagiarize other developers' projects and claim them as your own.
terms.section1_list3: Use automated bots to manipulate upvotes, views, or leaderboard rankings.
terms.section2_intro: Your intellectual property is sacred to us:
terms.section2_list1: Your Content: You retain all rights to the code and assets you link or upload.
terms.section2_list2: License to Us: You grant us a non-exclusive license to display, parse, and promote your project metadata on the platform.
terms.section2_list3: Open Source: If you mark your project as open-source, you agree to respect the specific OSI-approved license you choose.
terms.section2_outro: In short: What's yours is yours. We just provide the stage.
terms.section3_desc1: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms, engage in vote manipulation, or harass other users.
terms.section3_desc2: If your account is terminated, your projects will be removed from the active Leaderboard and Recruitment Radar permanently.
terms.section4_desc:
CS-Arena is provided 'as is'. We are not responsible for any career outcomes, server downtime, or disputes between project leads and recruited team members.

Use the platform to build and connect, but you assume all associated risks.
